title: ApprovalEvidence
description: Forge-proven eligible-approval evidence for a require-review obligation (ADR-0017 §3, OQ-23). One record is per approval rule (dossier §4), carrying the forge threshold (approvalsRequired), the eligible set, and the actual approvers (approvedBy[]). This is the only contract require-review may be satisfied against — a resolved discussion/challenge thread is acknowledgement, not authorization, and has no representable path into this schema. Safety-bearing: unknown fields reject at every level (ADR-0017 §9). pins is a cross-file $ref to DecisionRecord.$defs.pins — one shape only (roast P1-B).

approvalsRequired.description: Forge rule threshold (GitLab dossier §4 step (a): approval_rules[].approvals_required). Required when verifyingCapability is not none — a multi-approval require-review gate is otherwise unprovable (roast P1-A).

approvedBy.description: Actual approvers for this rule (GitLab dossier §4 step (c): approval_state.rules[].approved_by[]). Distinct from the eligible set. Required when verifyingCapability is not none.

pins.description: Canonical DecisionRecord pins $def (cross-file $ref). Approval evidence is only valid for the exact source/target/merge-result/tool/policy state it was observed against (ADR-0017 §1). Push-staleness is gated by pins.sourceSha (dossier §4 Staleness); no separate approved_at field — pins.sourceSha + observedAt subsume the dossier pairing for v1alpha1.

observedAt.description: When this evidence was fetched from the forge.

expiresAt.description: Optional. Present only when a genuine time-bound applies (e.g. require_reauthentication). GitLab approvals are push-invalidated (reset_approvals_on_push), already captured by pins.sourceSha — do not invent a timestamp for push-staleness (roast P2-D).

verifyingCapability.description: The forge capability that proved this evidence. "none" is the documented capability-gap value (D-017 B5): missing capability must never auto-merge and must never silently downgrade to a challenge/thread proxy — it is representable only as the fail-closed shape enforced below.

then.description: Capability gap: principal/source/eligibility/approvalsRequired/approvedBy must be structurally absent so a DecisionRecord can only represent fail-closed (D-017 B5).

id.description: Forge-stable identity of the primary observed approver (GitLab dossier §4: approved_by[].user.id). Multi-approval satisfaction is expressed via approvedBy[] + approvalsRequired, not by omitting the threshold.

isAuthor.description: REQUIRED. Adapter must positively assert the principal is not the MR author (const false). Author/committer exclusion remains an adapter invariant the schema cannot verify against an in-record author id — omitting isAuthor is invalid so the check cannot be silently skipped (roast P2-C).
REQUIRED on every actual approver entry. Same adapter-invariant honesty as principal.isAuthor (roast P2-C).

rule.description: The approval rule name or CODEOWNERS entry that proved eligibility (GitLab dossier §4 step (a)).

ruleType.description: Closed set of forge approval-rule kinds (GitLab dossier §4, rules[].rule_type). Deliberately excludes any discussion/challenge-resolution kind: ADR-0017 §3 treats a resolved discussion as acknowledgement, never authorization.

eligibleApproverIds.description: Forge-proven eligible-approver set for source.rule (GitLab dossier §4 step (b)). Must never be populated from a rule GitLab auto-approved because it was impossible to satisfy — dossier §4 invalid-rule pitfall.
